title: The True Hometown Blueprint
fact: The fictional town of Morioh is heavily based on Hirohiko Araki's real-world hometown of Sendai in Miyagi Prefecture. He modeled the town's rapid urban expansion and cozy-yet-uncanny residential layouts directly off his childhood memories.

title: The Aquarium Construction Layout
fact: Green Dolphin Street Prison is explicitly nicknamed 'The Aquarium' because it is a maximum-security complex entirely surrounded by ocean water on an isolated island off the coast of Florida, making physical escape virtually impossible.

title: The Sentient Moving Desert Topography
fact: The Devil's Palm is a dangerous, shifting geographical anomaly in the North American desert that acts as the Steel Ball Run universe's equivalent to the Stand Arrow. It grants supernatural abilities to those who survive its sudden, localized path alterations.

title: The Authentic Cave Canem Mosaic
fact: The ancient ruins of Pompeii featured in the Illuso fight are rendered with extreme historical accuracy. The specific location where the team retrieves the key is the real-world House of the Tragic Poet, famous for its preserved 'Cave Canem' (Beware of the Dog) floor mosaic.

title: The Dark Medieval Coal Town
fact: Windknight's Lot, the fog-covered town where Jonathan tracks DIO, was designed as a historical coal-mining settlement. Araki chose this location to evoke a gritty, classic Victorian horror aesthetic inspired by Jack the Ripper lore.

title: The Soft Rock Training Grounds
fact: The island fortress in Venice where Joseph and Caesar undergo their brutal Hamon conditioning is named Air Supplena Island. The location's name is a direct, stylized reference to the popular 1980s Australian soft rock duo Air Supply.

title: The Street-By-Street Cairo Mapping
fact: To track down the exact location of DIO’s mansion in Egypt, the production staff traveled to Cairo to map out real city blocks. The high-speed car chase against Pet Shop and the rooftop jumps during the time-stop sequence correspond to actual Cairo street grids.

title: The Architectural Missing Dimension
fact: The Ghost Girl's Alley is a hidden geographical pocket that does not exist on Morioh's official town registry map. It can only be entered by walking into a highly specific, otherwise mundane space between the convenience store and the residential walls.

title: The Isolation of San Giorgio Maggiore
fact: The Boss intentionally chooses the island church of San Giorgio Maggiore in Venice for his meeting with Bucciarati because its isolated bell tower lacks easy exit routes, transforming a beautiful historical landmark into a perfect psychological trap.

title: The Inverted Gravitational Epicenter
fact: The Kennedy Space Center at Cape Canaveral, Florida is chosen as the absolute climax location of Part 6 because the high concentration of cosmic and planetary trajectory data makes it the geographical catalyst point needed to trigger the evolution of Pucci’s Stand.

title: The Surreal Hong Kong Theme Park
fact: When the Crusaders meet Jean-Pierre Polnareff in Hong Kong, they fight at the real-life Tiger Balm Garden. This public theme park was world-famous for its surreal, brightly painted concrete statues depicting scenes from traditional Chinese mythology.

title: The Culinary Boundary Expansion
fact: Trattoria Trussardi, the Italian restaurant run by Tonio, is built on the absolute edge of Morioh's town borders, resting right next to the graveyard. This isolated positioning initially causes the protagonists to suspect it is a front for an enemy Stand user.

title: The Tectonic Fault Line Anomalies
fact: Following the real-world 2011 Tohoku earthquake, the alternate-universe version of Morioh develops the 'Wall Eyes.' These massive, subterranean earth structures erupt from the ground, possessing a unique geographical trait that swaps the properties of objects buried within them.

title: The Roman Vampire Coliseum
fact: The ancient vampire chariot race between Joseph and Wamuu takes place at the Skeleton Heel Stone in Switzerland. This massive, hidden subterranean arena was constructed centuries ago by the Pillar Men's vampire thralls specifically to host superhuman blood sports.

title: The Aristocratic British Hearth
fact: The iconic Joestar Mansion in Liverpool was meticulously styled after classic late-19th-century English country manors. Its massive central staircase and heavy stone hearth were deliberately designed to create a sense of aristocratic stability before being utterly incinerated.

title: The Subconscious Carnival Nightmare
fact: The surreal, neon amusement park featured in the Death 13 arc does not exist in the physical world. It is a persistent dream-space location completely generated by the subconscious mind of Mannish Boy, meaning regular human rules of geometry and physics do not apply.

title: The Sun-Drenched Island Fortune
fact: The public restroom where Polpo hid his multi-billion lire treasure is located on the beautiful real-world island of Capri. Araki chose this heavily touristed, bright Mediterranean destination to contrast against the gritty, urban Naples underworld of the opening episodes.

title: The Indestructible Steel Territory
fact: The electrical transmission tower containing the Stand Superfly is a permanent landmark on the outskirts of Morioh. Its current occupant bought the decommissioned steel structure for a cheap price to live completely off the grid, transforming a public utility into a self-sustaining home.

title: The Dead Zone Marshlands
fact: The Ultra Security House Unit within Green Dolphin Street Prison is built directly over a swampy, alligator-infested Florida wetland marsh. The oppressive humidity and wet terrain are leveraged by the prison staff to naturally deter any physical escape attempts.

title: The Extortionist Ginkgo Alley
fact: Shakedown Road is a unique neighborhood street in Morioh where the residents utilize the supernatural properties of the local Ginkgo trees. The fallen leaves slide objects around at high speeds, allowing the locals to pull elaborate insurance scams on unsuspecting visitors.

title: The Transcontinental Finish Line
fact: The multi-stage Steel Ball Run race begins on the sandy coastline of San Diego, California, and concludes on the bustling urban streets of New York City, mapping a complete geographical cross-section of late-19th-century American landscapes.

title: The Frozen Canals of Venice
fact: The high-speed highway battle against Ghiaccio takes place on the real-world Liberty Bridge (Ponte della Libertà), the singular coastal road track that connects the historic island city of Venice directly to the Italian mainland.

title: The Illusory Graveyard Village
fact: The mountain town in Pakistan where the Crusaders encounter Enya appears as a cozy, hospitable village but is entirely an illusion generated by the Stand Justice. The physical location is actually a massive, decrepit graveyard populated solely by reanimated corpses.

title: The Miraculous Coastal Landmark
fact: Boing Boing Cape is an official landmark on the coast of Morioh named after Yukako Yamagishi’s fall. The jagged rocks at the bottom became famous because a thick mattress of sentient hair naturally softened her impact, preventing any injury.

title: The Ancient Coliseum Stand Intersection
fact: The historic Roman Coliseum is chosen by an older Polnareff as the ultimate rendezvous point for Team Bucciarati. He selects this massive stone structure because its open, circular architectural layout makes it incredibly difficult for the Boss to stage a stealth attack.
